rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 23, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER WOOD ROBBINS, LLP. Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award For Judgment In Conformance Therewith. Petitioner Wood Robbins, LLP's petition for order confirming arbitration award is confirmed. Wood Robbins is entitled to entry of judgment in its favor and against respondents SMRWC, Inc. and BO-MIC, Inc. for the amounts awarded by the arbitrator. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Wood Robbins is required to prepare a proposed order and a proposed judgment which includes an amount of prejudgment through the date of the hearing and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SUCCESSFULMATCH.COM VS. KRONENBERGER ROSENFELD, LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant/Cross-Complainant Mcgreevey & Hele, Llp'S And Peter R. Mcgreevy'S Motion For Discharge And Dismissal Of Mcgrevey Parties From Interpleader Claim, Reasonable Fees And Costs And Severance And Or Stay Of The Interpleader. (Specially Set At 9:00a.M Per Ex Parte Order Of 4-16-2018)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 23, 2018, Line 13, Defendant/Cross-Complainant McGreevey & Hele, LLP's And Peter R. McGreevy's Motion For Discharge And Dismissal Of McGrevey Parties From Interpleader Claim, Reasonable Fees And Costs And Severance And Or Stay Of The Interpleader. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Defendants McGreevey & Henle, LLP and Peter McGreevey's motion for discharge and dismissal from the interpleader action, reasonable fees and costs, injunctive relief and severance and/or stay of the interpleader action is granted in part. The cross-complaint in interpleader and the cross-complaint filed by Paul Frasetto and his law firm and all issues related thereto are severed from this case and shall be included in the recently filed lawsuit by plaintiff Successfulmatch.com which has been assigned number CGC-18-565825. Any and all issues regarding the McGreevey parties' entitlement to fees and costs out of the funds held by Mr. McGrane and his law firm, the Frasetto parties' entitlement to any of those funds, and any other monetary claims asserted by the McGreevey parties, the Frasetto parties, the McGrane parties and Succsessfulmatch.com relating to the settlement monies paid by the McGreevey parties shall be resolved in case 565825 and not in this case. The effect of this order is to limit this case to the claims asserted by Successfulmatch.com against the Kronenberger parties and allow those claims to proceed to trial forthwith without interference from or delay caused by the collateral litigation resulting from the Frasetto parties' assertion of line or other rights to the settlement funds paid by the McGreevey parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the McGreevey parties is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: AMITABHO CHATTOPADHYAY VS. MICHAEL ROWELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Amitabho Chattopadhyay'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victoria Galea To Fifth And Sixth Set Of Requests For Production And For Monetary Sanction **[Part 3 Of 3]**
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 23, 2018, Line 1 [Part 3 of 3]: PLAINTIFF AMITABHO CHATTOPADHYAY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victoria Galea To Fifth And Sixth Set Of Requests For Production And For Monetary Sanction. Set 6, Request 5 (communications and other documents regarding long-term business strategy relating to the rental property): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled. The requested material is relevant to the matters at issue in the case. Defendant waived the objection that the request improperly seeks punitive damages discovery, however, had it been timely, the objection would be overruled because the items requested are relevant to issues in the case and not necessarily only pertinent to defendant's financial assets for punitive damages purposes. To the extent any responsive documents are communications between defendant and her attorney, or an attorney's work product, such material may be withheld, in which case defendant shall provide a Privilege Log generally describing any and all withheld documents. Defendant is ordered to provide a further verified written response consistent with this order and any Privilege Log within 7 days of the date of this order, and to produce any and all responsive documents in her possession also within 7 days from the date of this order. Request for Punitive Damages: No substantial justification has been offered by defendant for her meritless objections or her opposition to this motion. The failure to deliver any responsive documents whatsoever - such as the Rent Board decisions - and thereby limit the scope of this motion appears to be an obstruction of the discovery process. Punitive damages in the amount $711 is granted to plaintiff against defendant's attorneys and defendant, for plaintiff's cited expenses. IT IS SO ORDERED Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. [End of Part 3 of 3.] =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AMITABHO CHATTOPADHYAY VS. MICHAEL ROWELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victoria Galea To Fifth And Sixth Set Of Requests For Production And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 23, 2018, Line 1 [Part 1 of 3]: PLAINTIFF AMITABHO CHATTOPADHYAY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victoria Galea To Fifth And Sixth Set Of Requests For Production And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff moves to compel further written responses to document requests Set 5, Request 1 and Set 6, Requests 1-5. Set 5, Request 1 (copies of Rent Board decisions): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled. The requested material is relevant, non-privileged, and within defendant's possession according to her written response. Defendant is ordered to provide a further verified written response without objections within 7 days of the date of this order, and to produce any and all responsive documents in her possession also within 7 days from the date of this order. Set 6, Request 1 (valuation of rental property): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled. The requested material is relevant to the matters at issue in the case. Defendant waived the objection that the request improperly seeks punitive damages discovery, however, had it been timely, the objection would be overruled because the items requested are relevant to issues in the case and not necessarily only pertinent to defendant's financial assets for punitive damages purposes. To the extent any responsive documents are communications between defendant and her attorney, or an attorney's work product, such material may be withheld, in which case defendant shall provide a Privilege Log generally describing any and all withheld documents. Defendant is ordered to provide a further verified written response consistent with this order and any Privilege Log within 7 days of the date of this order, and to produce any and all responsive documents in her possession also within 7 days from the date of this order. [End of Part 1 of 3.] =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WELLHAUSEN VS. PHILIP JACOBSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff, David Wellhausen'S Motion To Quash. (Per Ost Filed 04-16-2018)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 23, 2018, Line 14, Plaintiff, David Wellhausen's Motion To Quash. Plaintiff David Wellhausen's motion to quash the subpoena served on Laura Hutto is granted in substantial part and Mr. Wellhausen is awarded monetary sanctions of $2890. The subpoena to Ms. Hutto is limited to all documents related to Mr. Wellhausen's left shoulder for the time period of April 9, 2005 to the present. The subpoena is overbroad. By filing this case, Mr. Wellhausen effected a waiver of his medical privacy only as to the medical condition he seeks to hold defendants liable. Mr. Wellhausen's privacy interest in his medical information outweighs defendants' interest in seeking the information based on speculation that relevant information might be discovered. The positon of defendants as to the scope of the subpoena lacks substantial justification. The court determines that the high end of reasonable fees and costs for this simple motion and the preceding meet and confer is $2890, calculated as eight hours of attorney time at $350 per hour and costs of $90. Monetary sanctions above that amount are excessive and would be punitive, which is contrary to the cost-shifting purpose of discovery monetary sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Wellhausen is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: STONEBRAE CLUB PARTNERS, LLC VS. ROBERT WOLLACK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 23, 2018, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT STONEBRAE CLUB PARTNERS, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant Stonebrae Club Partners, LLC's demurrer to all eight causes of action the in the first amended cross-complaint filed is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for violation of the CLRA and is overruled as to all other causes of action. The first through sixth and eighth causes of action for intentional misrepresentation, negligence misrepresentation, breach of contract, common count, and violations of the Unfair Competition Law, False Advertising Law, and Penal Code 493(c) are all adequately alleged. Many of Stonebrae's arguments raise factual issues not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. The FACC adequately alleges that Stonebrae misrepresented the cost of membership and that it is a private club to Mr. Wollack. Whether these representations were intentionally or negligently false and whether Mr. Wollack's reliance was reasonable are improper arguments at the demurrer stage. The FACC adequately alleges that Stonebrae breached the Membership Agreement by not providing a private club at the cost Mr. Wollack initially agreed. The common count sufficiently alleges that Stonebrae owes Mr. Wollack approximately $12,000. The UCL and FAL are properly pled and allow Mr. Wollack to recover monetary relief, even if injunctive relief may not be proper. The civil claim for violation of section 493(c) sufficiently alleges that Stonebrae obtained Mr. Wollack's money under false pretenses by misrepresenting itself to be a private club. Stonebrae's arguments that the money was never stolen and there was no false pretense are improper arguments at the demurrer stage. Mr. Wollack cannot seek injunctive relief under the CLRA claim because he is no longer a member of the club and does not suffer ongoing threat of harm by Stonebrae's alleged CLRA violations. Mr. Wollack is unable to amend his CLRA claim to seek monetary relief because he failed to provide a pre-suit notice to Stonebrae within 30 days of filing the cross-complaint. (CLRA 1782(a); CLRA 1782(d).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Mr. Wollack is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
